pattern: Backtracking up the pyramid: for each adjacent pair on the current row, try every allowed top block, recursing only when a full next row can be assembled, with memoisation on solved rows.
hints: Index the allowed patterns by their two-character base so you can quickly list which tops can sit on a given pair.
Build the next row left to right: each position depends only on the pair of blocks directly below it, and a row of length one means success.
Recurse from the bottom upward, trying each candidate top for every pair and backtracking on dead ends; cache rows already proven impossible to avoid repeating work.

edge_inputs.description: empty allowed list and minimal base cannot build
single pattern that completes a two-block base
pattern exists but wrong orientation fails
multiple choices but one branch needed succeeds

stress.description: small random bases with random allowed pattern subsets
stress.generator_python:
import itertools
def gen(rng, size):
    alphabet = 'ABCDEF'
    blen = rng.randint(2, 6)
    bottom = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(blen))
    all_patterns = [a + b + c for a in alphabet for b in alphabet for c in alphabet]
    rng.shuffle(all_patterns)
    m = rng.randint(0, 60)
    allowed = all_patterns[:m]
    return [bottom, allowed]
stress.note: small base keeps the backtracking search bounded
